original: A new survey by Harvard University finds more than two-thirds of young Americans disapprove of President Trump’s use of Twitter.
translation_literal: 哈佛大学的一项新调查发现，超过三分之二的年轻美国人不赞成特朗普总统使用 Twitter。

chunks.english: A new survey by Harvard University
chunks.chinese: 哈佛大学的一项新调查

chunks.english: more than two-thirds of young Americans disapprove of President Trump’s use of Twitter
chunks.chinese: 超过三分之二的年轻美国人不赞成特朗普总统使用 Twitter

chunks.english: more than two-thirds of young Americans
chunks.chinese: 超过三分之二的年轻美国人

chunks.relation: 作 disapprove of 的宾语

original: The implication is that Millennials prefer news from the White House to be filtered through other sources, not a president’s social media platform.
translation_literal: 其含义是，千禧一代更喜欢来自白宫的新闻通过其他来源过滤，而不是通过总统的社交媒体平台。

chunks.english: that Millennials prefer news from the White House to be filtered through other sources, not a president’s social media platform
chunks.chinese: 千禧一代更喜欢来自白宫的新闻通过其他来源过滤，而不是通过总统的社交媒体平台

chunks.english: to be filtered through other sources
chunks.relation: 补充说明 news 的接收方式

chunks.english: not a president’s social media platform
chunks.chinese: 而不是总统的社交媒体平台
chunks.relation: 对 other sources 作对比说明

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: prefer + 宾语 + to be done
sentence_patterns.explanation: prefer 的宾语 news 后接不定式被动补足语，说明新闻应如何被过滤。

original: Most Americans rely on social media to check daily headlines.
translation_literal: 大多数美国人依靠社交媒体查看每日头条。

chunks.relation: 作 rely on 的宾语
说明 rely on social media 的目的

original: Yet as distrust has risen toward all media, people may be starting to beef up their media literacy skills.
translation_literal: 然而，随着对所有媒体的不信任已经上升，人们可能正开始加强自己的媒介素养技能。

chunks.english: as distrust has risen toward all media
chunks.chinese: 随着对所有媒体的不信任已经上升

chunks.english: to beef up their media literacy skills
chunks.chinese: 加强自己的媒介素养技能

sentence_patterns.explanation: as 在这里表示“随着”，引出背景变化。

original: Such a trend is badly needed.
translation_literal: 这样的趋势是非常需要的。

original: During the 2016 presidential campaign, nearly a quarter of web content shared by Twitter users in the politically critical state of Michigan was fake news, according to the University of Oxford.
translation_literal: 根据牛津大学的说法，在 2016 年总统竞选期间，在政治上关键的密歇根州，Twitter 用户分享的网络内容中近四分之一是假新闻。

chunks.chinese: 在 2016 年总统竞选期间

chunks.english: nearly a quarter of web content shared by Twitter users in the politically critical state of Michigan
chunks.chinese: 在政治上关键的密歇根州由 Twitter 用户分享的网络内容中近四分之一

chunks.english: shared by Twitter users in the politically critical state of Michigan
chunks.chinese: 由政治上关键的密歇根州的 Twitter 用户分享的

chunks.english: according to the University of Oxford

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 长主语 + was + 表语 + 来源插入
sentence_patterns.explanation: 主语很长，核心是 nearly a quarter was fake news。

original: And a survey conducted for BuzzFeed News found 44 percent of Facebook users rarely or never trust news from the media giant.
translation_literal: 并且一项为 BuzzFeed News 进行的调查发现，44% 的 Facebook 用户很少或从不信任来自这个媒体巨头的新闻。

chunks.english: a survey conducted for BuzzFeed News
chunks.chinese: 一项为 BuzzFeed News 进行的调查
为 BuzzFeed News 进行的

chunks.english: 44 percent of Facebook users rarely or never trust news from the media giant
chunks.chinese: 44% 的 Facebook 用户很少或从不信任来自这个媒体巨头的新闻
来自这个媒体巨头的新闻

original: Young people who are digital natives are indeed becoming more skillful at separating fact from fiction in cyberspace.
translation_literal: 作为数字原住民的年轻人确实正在变得更善于在网络空间中区分事实与虚构。

chunks.english: Young people who are digital natives
chunks.chinese: 作为数字原住民的年轻人

chunks.english: more skillful at separating fact from fiction in cyberspace
chunks.chinese: 更善于在网络空间中区分事实与虚构
chunks.relation: 作 are becoming 的表语

chunks.english: at separating fact from fiction in cyberspace
chunks.chinese: 在网络空间中区分事实与虚构方面

original: A Knight Foundation focus-group survey of young people between ages 14 and 24 found they use “distributed trust” to verify stories.
translation_literal: 奈特基金会对 14 至 24 岁年轻人进行的一项焦点小组调查发现，他们使用“分布式信任”来核实新闻。

chunks.english: A Knight Foundation focus-group survey of young people between ages 14 and 24
chunks.chinese: 奈特基金会对 14 至 24 岁年轻人进行的一项焦点小组调查

chunks.english: of young people between ages 14 and 24
chunks.chinese: 关于 14 至 24 岁年轻人的

chunks.english: they use “distributed trust” to verify stories
chunks.chinese: 他们使用分布式信任来核实新闻

original: They cross-check sources and prefer news from different perspectives — especially those that are open about any bias.
translation_literal: 他们交叉核对来源，并偏好来自不同视角的新闻，尤其是那些公开说明任何偏见的新闻来源。

chunks.english: especially those that are open about any bias
chunks.chinese: 尤其是那些公开说明任何偏见的新闻来源
chunks.relation: 补充说明 prefer 的新闻来源

chunks.english: that are open about any bias

sentence_patterns.explanation: especially those... 补充说明他们偏爱的来源类型。

original: “Many young people assume a great deal of personal responsibility for educating themselves and actively seeking out opposing viewpoints,” the survey concluded.
translation_literal: 调查总结道：“许多年轻人认为自己对教育自己和主动寻找相反观点负有很大的个人责任。”

chunks.english: a great deal of personal responsibility
for educating themselves and actively seeking out opposing viewpoints
chunks.chinese: 对于教育自己和主动寻找相反观点

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 直接引语 + the survey concluded
sentence_patterns.explanation: the survey concluded 是引语来源。
for 后两个动作并列说明责任内容。

original: Such active research can have another effect.
translation_literal: 这种主动研究可能产生另一个影响。

original: A 2014 survey conducted in Australia, Britain, and the United States by the University of Wisconsin-Madison found that young people’s reliance on social media led to greater political engagement.
translation_literal: 威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校在澳大利亚、英国和美国进行的一项 2014 年调查发现，年轻人对社交媒体的依赖导致了更大的政治参与。

chunks.english: A 2014 survey conducted in Australia, Britain, and the United States by the University of Wisconsin-Madison
chunks.chinese: 威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校在澳大利亚、英国和美国进行的一项 2014 年调查

chunks.english: conducted in Australia, Britain, and the United States by the University of Wisconsin-Madison
chunks.chinese: 由威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校在澳大利亚、英国和美国进行的

chunks.english: that young people’s reliance on social media led to greater political engagement
chunks.chinese: 年轻人对社交媒体的依赖导致了更大的政治参与

chunks.english: young people’s reliance on social media
chunks.chinese: 年轻人对社交媒体的依赖

chunks.relation: 作 led to 的宾语

original: Social media allows users to experience news events more intimately and immediately while also permitting them to re-share news as a projection of their values and interests.
translation_literal: 社交媒体允许用户更亲密、更即时地体验新闻事件，同时也允许他们把新闻作为自身价值观和兴趣的投射来再次分享。

chunks.english: to experience news events more intimately and immediately
chunks.chinese: 更亲密、更即时地体验新闻事件
chunks.relation: 补充说明 users 做什么

chunks.english: while also permitting them to re-share news as a projection of their values and interests
chunks.chinese: 同时也允许他们把新闻作为自身价值观和兴趣的投射来再次分享
chunks.relation: 说明 social media 的并行动能
while 从句省略主语 social media 后的谓语

chunks.english: to re-share news as a projection of their values and interests
chunks.chinese: 把新闻作为自身价值观和兴趣的投射来再次分享
chunks.relation: 补充说明 them 做什么
while 从句省略的主语，承接主句 Social media

sentence_patterns.explanation: allows 和 permitting 说明社交媒体两个并行作用。

original: This forces users to be more conscious of their role in passing along information.
translation_literal: 这迫使用户更加意识到自己在传递信息中的角色。

chunks.english: to be more conscious of their role in passing along information
chunks.chinese: 更加意识到自己在传递信息中的角色
chunks.relation: 补充说明 users 被迫做什么

original: A survey by Barna research group found the top reason given by Americans for the fake news phenomenon is “reader error,” more so than made-up stories or factual mistakes in reporting.
translation_literal: 巴纳研究集团的一项调查发现，美国人为假新闻现象给出的首要原因是“读者错误”，比编造的报道或报道中的事实错误更是如此。

chunks.english: A survey by Barna research group
chunks.chinese: 巴纳研究集团的一项调查

chunks.english: the top reason given by Americans for the fake news phenomenon is “reader error,” more so than made-up stories or factual mistakes in reporting
chunks.chinese: 美国人为假新闻现象给出的首要原因是读者错误，比编造故事或报道事实错误更是如此

chunks.english: the top reason given by Americans for the fake news phenomenon
chunks.chinese: 美国人为假新闻现象给出的首要原因

chunks.english: given by Americans for the fake news phenomenon
chunks.chinese: 由美国人为假新闻现象给出的

chunks.english: more so than made-up stories or factual mistakes in reporting
chunks.chinese: 比编造的报道或报道中的事实错误更是如此
chunks.relation: 补充比较 reader error 的重要性

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: found + 宾语从句 + 比较插入
sentence_patterns.explanation: 宾语从句主语 reason 带后置定语，句尾 more so than... 作比较补充。

original: About a third say the problem of fake news lies in “misinterpretation or exaggeration of actual news” via social media.
translation_literal: 大约三分之一的人说，假新闻的问题在于通过社交媒体对真实新闻的“误解或夸大”。

chunks.english: the problem of fake news lies in “misinterpretation or exaggeration of actual news” via social media
chunks.chinese: 假新闻的问题在于通过社交媒体对真实新闻的误解或夸大

chunks.english: “misinterpretation or exaggeration of actual news” via social media
chunks.chinese: 通过社交媒体对真实新闻的误解或夸大
chunks.relation: 作 lies in 的宾语

original: In other words, the choice to share news on social media may be the heart of the issue.
translation_literal: 换句话说，在社交媒体上分享新闻的选择可能是问题的核心。

chunks.english: the choice to share news on social media
chunks.chinese: 在社交媒体上分享新闻的选择

chunks.english: to share news on social media
chunks.chinese: 在社交媒体上分享新闻的

chunks.relation: 作 may be 的表语

original: “This indicates there is a real personal responsibility in counteracting this problem,” says Roxanne Stone, editor in chief at Barna Group.
translation_literal: 巴纳集团主编罗克珊·斯通说：“这表明，在抵制这个问题方面存在真正的个人责任。”

chunks.english: there is a real personal responsibility in counteracting this problem
chunks.chinese: 在抵制这个问题方面存在真正的个人责任

chunks.english: there is a real personal responsibility
chunks.relation: 宾语从句内部 there be 结构
there be 结构真实主语

chunks.english: says Roxanne Stone, editor in chief at Barna Group
chunks.chinese: 巴纳集团主编罗克珊·斯通说

chunks.english: editor in chief at Barna Group
chunks.relation: 解释 Roxanne Stone 的身份
there be 结构中宾语从句内部谓语

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 直接引语 + says + 发言人

sentence_patterns.explanation: editor in chief... 解释 Roxanne Stone 的身份。

original: So when young people are critical of an over-tweeting president, they reveal a mental discipline in thinking skills — and in their choices on when to share on social media.
translation_literal: 所以，当年轻人批评一位过度发推的总统时，他们展现出思维技能上的一种心理纪律，以及在何时在社交媒体上分享方面的选择纪律。

chunks.english: when young people are critical of an over-tweeting president
chunks.chinese: 当年轻人批评一位过度发推的总统时
chunks.relation: 说明主句 reveal 的时间背景

chunks.chinese: 批评一位过度发推的总统

chunks.english: a mental discipline in thinking skills
chunks.chinese: 思维技能上的一种心理纪律

chunks.english: and in their choices on when to share on social media
chunks.chinese: 以及在何时在社交媒体上分享方面的选择
chunks.relation: 与 in thinking skills 并列补充 discipline 的体现

chunks.english: when to share on social media
chunks.chinese: 何时在社交媒体上分享
chunks.relation: 作 choices on 的宾语

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: when 从句 + 主句 + 破折号补充
sentence_patterns.explanation: when 从句给出语境，破折号后补充 mental discipline 的第二个体现。
